cloudLogin.title: Link your account
cloudLogin.description: Enter your Novabot (LFI) cloud credentials. We'll automatically import your mower and charging station into the local server.
cloudLogin.skipHint: No account? You can create one manually in the dashboard later.
cloudLogin.noDevices: No devices found on this account.
cloudLogin.successDesc: Your devices are linked. Open the Novabot app and log in with this email and password.
cloudLogin.errorLogin: Login failed. Check your email and password.
cloudLogin.errorNetwork: Cannot connect to LFI cloud. Check your internet connection.
cloudLogin.existingDesc: The server already has an account configured. You can use it or import a different one from the cloud.

welcome.title: Welcome to OpenNova
welcome.subtitle: Take back control of your robot mower
welcome.description: This wizard guides you through setting up OpenNova — an open-source replacement for the Novabot cloud. After setup, your mower runs fully offline with its own local server and dashboard.

firmware.title: Select firmware
firmware.description: Upload the OpenNova firmware file. Expected name: {file}
firmware.invalidFormat: Invalid file format. Expected: .deb firmware file.
firmware.uploadError: Error during upload. Please try again.
firmware.dropSubtitle: or click to select a file

network.title: Configure network
network.description: Select the network your mower is connected to. The mower will download the firmware via this IP address.
network.loadError: Cannot load network interfaces. Is the bootstrap server still running?
network.noInterfaces: No network interfaces found. Check your WiFi connection.
network.dnsAutoDesc: The Docker container includes a DNS server. Set this IP as DNS server on your router:

docker.title: Start Docker server
docker.description: The OpenNova server runs in a Docker container. It contains the MQTT broker, dashboard, and DNS.
docker.notInstalledDesc: Download and install Docker Desktop for your system:
docker.notRunningDesc: Start Docker Desktop and wait for the green icon to appear. The wizard checks automatically every 5 seconds.
docker.errorHealthTimeout: Health check timeout (60s) — check Docker logs: docker logs opennova

wait.title: Waiting for mower
wait.description: The wizard is waiting for your mower to connect to the local MQTT broker. Follow the steps below.
wait.instruction: Turn on the mower and check WiFi connection
wait.firmwareStockHint: Stock firmware detected. OTA update will install OpenNova.
wait.pathAutoTitle: Mower was already paired to the cloud?
wait.pathAutoDesc: Just turn it on — it will connect automatically via DNS.
wait.step1: Create an account in the dashboard
wait.step1Sub: Go to http://{ip} and register a new account (not your LFI cloud account!)
wait.step2: Open the Novabot app → log in with your new account
wait.step2Sub: The app will automatically connect to your local server via DNS
wait.step3: Tap "+" → Add mower → complete the BLE pairing
wait.step3Sub: This sets the MQTT address in the mower to your local server
wait.step4: The wizard will automatically proceed once the mower connects
wait.importDesc: Enter your LFI cloud credentials — we'll import your mower and charging station automatically.
wait.importSuccessDesc: Account created and devices linked. Open the Novabot app and log in with this account.
wait.importNoDevices: No devices found on this account.
wait.importErrorLogin: Login failed. Check your email and password.
wait.importErrorNetwork: Cannot connect to LFI cloud. Check your internet connection.
wait.bleDesc: Redirect your mower to the local server via Bluetooth. No DNS or router changes needed.
wait.bleDone: MQTT address updated! Waiting for mower to reconnect...
wait.bleHint: Requires Chrome or Edge. Safari and Firefox don't support Web Bluetooth.
wait.bleUnavailableDesc: Web Bluetooth is not supported in this browser. Open this page in Chrome or Edge instead, or configure DNS on your router.
wait.bleWifiLabel: WiFi credentials (same network as the mower)
wait.bleWifiHint: WiFi credentials are required — the firmware needs them before accepting MQTT changes.
wait.bleNativeDesc: Native Bluetooth — more reliable than Web Bluetooth.

confirm.title: Confirm OTA flash
confirm.description: Review the details and click Flash now to start the firmware update.
confirm.sameVersionShort: The mower is already running the same version.
confirm.sameVersionMsg: The mower is already running {version}. Flash anyway? (e.g. to reinstall)
confirm.warnRestart: The mower will restart after downloading (~10-20 minutes)
confirm.warnNoPower: Do NOT turn off the mower during flashing
confirm.warnOffline: After reboot, the mower is self-contained (no Novabot cloud needed)
confirm.connectionError: Connection error. Check if the server is still running.
confirm.stockRebootMsg: The mower is running stock firmware (no SSH). After flashing, the mower may not reboot automatically. You will need to turn it off and back on manually.

progress.title: Flashing firmware...
progress.description: Do NOT close this window. The wizard automatically detects when the mower is ready.
progress.stage1Sub: Mower is downloading and installing firmware
progress.stage3Sub: Waiting for the new server to come online
progress.timeoutDesc: The wizard tried SSH recovery but the mower is still not back. The firmware is likely installed but the mower needs a full restart.
progress.sshDesc: The wizard is trying to recover the mower via SSH. Check the log above for details. If the mower doesn't come back: turn it off completely, wait 10 seconds, and turn it back on.
progress.stockPowerCycleTitle: Mower not responding — power cycle needed
progress.stockPowerCycleDesc: The mower is running stock firmware without SSH, so automatic recovery is not possible. Please power cycle the mower manually:
progress.stockPowerCycleHint: The wizard will automatically detect when the mower comes back online.

done.title: Done!
done.description: OpenNova firmware has been successfully installed on your mower.
done.reconnected: The mower has reconnected. Open the OpenNova dashboard to continue.
done.step1: Open the Novabot app — the mower connects automatically via the Docker server
done.step2: Draw a mowing map in the dashboard
done.step3: You can now close the Bootstrap tool
